agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using region-sovereign deployments with a thin global coordination layer. Each region (EU, US, APAC) owns its customer and operational data exclusively, enforcing data residency at the infrastructure level. A global mesh synchronizes only non-PII operational metrics and reference data. Region isolation is the primary architectural constraint — every design decision flows from it.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Region Sovereign Stack (EU/US/APAC)","responsibility":"Full application deployment per region: API gateway, application services, database, cache, message broker. Handles all customer-facing operations for that region's users and couriers. Each stack is independently deployable and operationally autonomous.","boundaries":"Hard boundary at region edge. No customer PII crosses this boundary. Region stack owns: customer records, order data, courier profiles, delivery assignments, payment references. Communicates outward only via sanitized event stream."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Routes requests to the local region stack. Enforces geo-affinity via DNS-based routing (latency-based + geofencing). Rejects or redirects requests that would violate data residency (e.g., US user accidentally hitting EU endpoint).","boundaries":"Entry point per region. Terminates TLS, enforces auth, applies rate limiting. No cross-region fan-out for customer operations."},{"name":"Regional Data Store (Primary)","responsibility":"PostgreSQL (or equivalent) with regional read replicas. Stores all customer PII, order data, courier data, delivery records. Encrypted at rest with region-specific KMS keys.","boundaries":"Never replicated outside its region. Backups stored within region. Access restricted to regional application services only."},{"name":"Regional Event Bus","responsibility":"Kafka/SQS per region for intra-region async processing: order lifecycle events, courier dispatch, notification triggers. Also publishes sanitized (PII-stripped) events to the Global Event Mesh.","boundaries":"Raw events stay in-region. Only sanitized projections (aggregated metrics, anonymized operational data) are forwarded to the global layer."},{"name":"Global Event Mesh","responsibility":"Thin cross-region event backbone (e.g., AWS EventBridge Global Endpoints or a dedicated Kafka MirrorMaker topology with PII filter). Carries: operational metrics, aggregated KPIs, reference data changes (e.g., global pricing rules, service catalog updates). Never carries PII.","boundaries":"Strict schema enforcement — events must conform to a PII-free schema validated at publish time. Acts as anti-corruption layer between regions."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard Service","responsibility":"Consumes sanitized metrics from all regions via the Global Event Mesh. Materializes near-real-time views: delivery volumes, SLA compliance, courier utilization, regional health. Deployed in a neutral region or multi-homed.","boundaries":"Read-only. Cannot write back to regional stacks. Operates on aggregated/anonymized data only. Acceptable latency: 5-15 seconds for metric freshness."},{"name":"Global Reference Data Service","responsibility":"Manages data that is legitimately global: service catalog, pricing tiers, courier certification types, SLA definitions. Publishes changes via Global Event Mesh; each region caches locally.","boundaries":"Owns only non-PII reference data. Regions pull and cache — never depend on synchronous calls to this service."},{"name":"Regional Courier Assignment Service","responsibility":"Assigns couriers to deliveries within the region. Enforces the hard constraint that couriers can only be assigned to deliveries in their registered operating region. Uses regional courier pool exclusively.","boundaries":"Region-scoped. No visibility into other regions' courier pools. Courier-region binding enforced at registration time and validated at assignment time."},{"name":"Regional Resilience Layer","responsibility":"Circuit breakers, local caches, and degraded-mode handlers. If the region's primary DB fails, serves read-only cached data for order tracking and customer info. Queues new orders for replay on recovery.","boundaries":"Operates entirely within the region. Degraded mode provides: order status lookup (from cache), estimated delivery info, read-only account access. Blocks: new order creation (queued), payment processing (deferred)."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Client Application","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"HTTPS REST/gRPC. DNS-based routing (Route53 latency + geofence policy) ensures clients hit their home region. JWT contains region claim validated at gateway.","coupling_type":"loose — client is region-unaware, DNS handles routing"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Region Sovereign Stack","contract":"Internal service mesh (mTLS). Gateway forwards authenticated requests to appropriate microservices within the region.","coupling_type":"tight within region (intentional) — services are co-deployed and share regional infrastructure"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Global Event Mesh","contract":"Publish sanitized events conforming to GlobalOperationalEvent schema. Fields: event_type, region_id, timestamp, metric_name, metric_value, anonymized_entity_ids (hashed). PII filter enforced by a gateway consumer that validates schema before forwarding.","coupling_type":"loose — fire-and-forget async, schema-validated, PII-stripped"},{"from":"Global Event Mesh","to":"Global Operations Dashboard Service","contract":"Subscribe to all regional metric streams. Dashboard materializes CQRS read models per region and global aggregates.","coupling_type":"loose — eventual consistency, read-only consumption"},{"from":"Global Reference Data Service","to":"Regional Event Bus","contract":"Publish ReferenceDataChanged events. Regions subscribe and update local cache. Idempotent — regions can re-request full snapshot on startup.","coupling_type":"loose — async, cache-based, regions function independently if global service is down"},{"from":"Regional Courier Assignment Service","to":"Regional Data Store","contract":"Query courier pool filtered by region + availability + proximity. Write delivery assignment records. Transactional consistency within the regional DB.","coupling_type":"tight — synchronous DB access within region (appropriate for consistency requirement)"}],"data_flow":"1) Customer request → DNS routes to nearest compliant region → Regional API Gateway authenticates and routes internally. 2) Order placed → Regional services process entirely within region → courier assigned from regional pool → delivery tracked regionally. 3) Regional Event Bus emits lifecycle events internally for async processing (notifications, analytics). 4) PII-stripping gateway consumer filters regional events → publishes sanitized metrics to Global Event Mesh. 5) Global Dashboard consumes from all three regional streams → materializes unified operational view. 6) Reference data changes flow from Global Reference Data Service → Global Event Mesh → Regional caches (async, eventually consistent).","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty / Region Isolation","rationale":"GDPR and CCPA mandate that customer data stays in-region. Rather than complex cross-region encryption schemes, we make the region the fundamental deployment and data boundary. This eliminates an entire class of compliance risks by making violations architecturally impossible."},{"pattern":"CQRS for Global Dashboard","rationale":"The global dashboard is a read-only projection of sanitized data from all regions. CQRS lets us build optimized read models without coupling the dashboard to regional write models or requiring cross-region queries."},{"pattern":"Event-Carried State Transfer (sanitized)","rationale":"Regions emit events carrying the operational state the global layer needs, avoiding synchronous cross-region calls. The PII-stripping at the boundary is the critical innovation — events are useful but safe."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead / Cell-Based Architecture","rationale":"Each region is an independent cell. A failure in EU does not cascade to US or APAC. This directly addresses the resilience requirement and is the natural pattern for multi-region with data sovereignty."},{"pattern":"Cache-Aside with Graceful Degradation","rationale":"Regional resilience layer caches hot data (order status, customer profile reads). On primary failure, serves stale-but-functional reads while queuing writes. Meets the degraded-but-functional requirement."},{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (PII Filter Gateway)","rationale":"The PII-stripping consumer at the region boundary acts as an ACL, ensuring the global context never receives data it should not have. Schema validation makes this enforceable and auditable."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Regional Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: OrderId — contains line items, delivery address, status, regional assignment)","Delivery (root: DeliveryId — courier assignment, route, tracking, proof of delivery)"]},{"name":"Regional Courier Operations","classification":"core","aggregates":["Courier (root: CourierId — profile, operating region, availability, certifications)","CourierAssignment (root: AssignmentId — links courier to delivery, enforces region constraint as invariant)"]},{"name":"Regional Customer Identity","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer (root: CustomerId — PII, preferences, region of registration)","CustomerAddress (value object — validated against region, never transmitted cross-region)"]},{"name":"Global Operations Intelligence","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionalMetricSnapshot (root: SnapshotId — point-in-time aggregated metrics per region, no PII)","GlobalKPI (root: KPIId — computed from cross-region snapshots)"]},{"name":"Global Reference Data","classification":"generic","aggregates":["ServiceCatalog (root: CatalogId — available service types, global)","PricingTier (root: TierId — pricing rules, may have regional overrides)","SLADefinition (root: SLAId — target delivery times, reliability targets)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Regional Order Management","downstream":"Regional Courier Operations","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Order Management publishes DeliveryRequested events, Courier Operations consumes and assigns. Order Management defines the delivery need; Courier Operations fulfills it."},{"upstream":"Regional Order Management","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — Regional events are PII-stripped before crossing into the global context. Global Intelligence conforms to its own sanitized schema."},{"upstream":"Regional Courier Operations","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — same PII-stripping pattern. Courier utilization metrics flow up, courier identity does not."},{"upstream":"Global Reference Data","downstream":"Regional Order Management","relationship":"Conformist — Regions conform to global reference data schemas (pricing, SLA definitions). Regions cache locally and tolerate staleness."},{"upstream":"Global Reference Data","downstream":"Regional Courier Operations","relationship":"Conformist — Courier certification types and service categories come from global reference data."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully independent regional stacks (no shared database, no cross-region replication of customer data)","gains":"Compliance by construction — data residency violations are architecturally impossible. Independent failure domains. Simple mental model: region = sovereign unit.","sacrifices":"A customer who moves from EU to US requires explicit data migration (not automatic). Global reporting is limited to aggregated/anonymized data. Higher infrastructure cost (3x base deployment).","alternatives_considered":["Shared database with row-level encryption and region tags — rejected: too easy to leak data through queries, joins, or backups. Compliance becomes a runtime enforcement problem rather than structural guarantee.","Single global deployment with data routing — rejected: single failure domain, complex data routing logic, compliance depends on correct software behavior rather than infrastructure isolation."]},{"decision":"PII-stripping ACL at region boundary rather than end-to-end encryption of cross-region events","gains":"Simpler global consumers — they never handle PII, so they need no PII-handling controls, no DPO review, no right-to-erasure logic. Reduces blast radius of a global-layer breach to zero PII exposure.","sacrifices":"Global dashboard cannot show customer-specific drill-downs. Debugging cross-region issues requires jumping to the source region's tools.","alternatives_considered":["Encrypted PII in global events with regional decryption keys — rejected: adds key management complexity, global services still technically 'process' PII even if encrypted, complicating GDPR Article 28 compliance."]},{"decision":"Degraded read-only mode via regional cache rather than cross-region failover","gains":"No data leaves the region even during failures. Much simpler than cross-region active-active replication. Customers still get order status and tracking.","sacrifices":"During regional outage: no new orders, no payment processing, no courier assignment. Recovery depends on regional infrastructure restoration.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region active-active with selective data replication — rejected: fundamentally conflicts with data residency requirements. Even temporary replication for failover puts EU data in US infrastructure.","Multi-AZ within region as primary resilience — recommended as complementary strategy, not a trade-off. Multi-AZ should be the baseline; cache-based degradation is the fallback when even multi-AZ fails."]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., Middle East, Latin America)","evolution_path":"The cell-based architecture makes adding a region a deployment exercise, not an architectural change. Deploy the sovereign stack to a new region, configure DNS routing, connect the sanitized event stream to the Global Event Mesh. Estimated effort: weeks, not months."},{"area":"Regional regulatory variations","evolution_path":"Each region's stack can be independently configured for local compliance requirements (data retention periods, consent management, audit logging) without affecting other regions."},{"area":"Global dashboard evolution","evolution_path":"CQRS read models can be extended independently. New metrics, new visualizations, new aggregation windows — all additive changes to the dashboard's materialized views without touching regional systems."},{"area":"Service granularity within regions","evolution_path":"Regional stacks can internally decompose from a modular monolith to microservices as needed, independently per region. The regional boundary is the stable interface; internal decomposition is a local decision."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Data residency boundary","reason":"This is rigid by design and by law. Customer PII cannot cross regional boundaries. This constraint is non-negotiable and is enforced structurally. Any feature that requires cross-region PII access must be redesigned to work without it."},{"area":"Courier-region binding","reason":"A courier's operating region is an invariant of the CourierAssignment aggregate. This is a hard business rule: couriers are licensed, insured, and legally authorized per region. This constraint should be enforced at the domain model level, not just validation."},{"area":"PII-stripping at region boundary","reason":"The ACL that strips PII from outbound events is a critical compliance control. It must be treated as security infrastructure: audited, tested, never bypassed. Schema validation must reject any event containing PII fields."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Clock skew and event ordering across regions causing dashboard inconsistencies","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use logical timestamps (vector clocks or Lamport timestamps) for event ordering in the global dashboard. Accept that cross-region metrics are eventually consistent with a published SLA (e.g., 15-second freshness). Do not attempt global total ordering — it's unnecessary and expensive."},{"risk":"PII leakage through the sanitization ACL due to schema evolution","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Implement allowlist-based sanitization (explicitly enumerate permitted fields) rather than denylist (strip known PII fields). New fields are blocked by default until explicitly classified. Automated compliance tests in CI that scan event schemas for PII patterns. Regular DPO audit of the allowlist."},{"risk":"Regional outage exceeding cache TTL, causing complete service loss","severity":"high","mitigation":"Implement tiered caching: hot cache (in-memory, 5-min TTL) + warm cache (Redis, 1-hour TTL) + cold cache (local disk snapshot, 24-hour TTL). Stale data is clearly marked in the UI. Multi-AZ deployment within each region is the primary defense; cache-based degradation is the secondary."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing 3+ independent deployments","severity":"high","mitigation":"Infrastructure-as-code with parameterized region modules. Centralized deployment orchestration (but regional execution). Unified observability platform that aggregates non-PII operational telemetry. Runbooks per region with region-specific escalation paths."},{"risk":"Latency budget exceeded for < 100ms requirement","severity":"medium","mitigation":"DNS-based routing ensures customers hit local region. Within-region latency budget: 20ms network + 30ms application + 40ms database + 10ms overhead. Requires regional CDN for static assets, connection pooling, prepared queries, and aggressive caching. Monitor p99 latency per region continuously."},{"risk":"Customer relocation across regions requires manual data migration","severity":"low","mitigation":"Build a documented, auditable data portability workflow: export from source region, transmit encrypted to customer (or directly to destination region with explicit consent), import at destination, verify, then delete at source. This satisfies GDPR Article 20 (data portability) and handles the rare relocation case without compromising the architecture."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1: Deploy the existing EU monolith as the EU sovereign stack. Phase 2: Extract the PII-stripping ACL and Global Event Mesh. Phase 3: Deploy US region as a clean sovereign stack (same codebase, new infrastructure). Phase 4: Build Global Operations Dashboard on CQRS read models. Phase 5: Deploy APAC. Each phase is independently valuable and shippable. Do not attempt a big-bang multi-region cutover.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/regions/eu/main.tf — EU regional infrastructure module","infra/regions/us/main.tf — US regional infrastructure module (parameterized from EU)","infra/regions/apac/main.tf — APAC regional infrastructure module","infra/modules/sovereign-stack/ — reusable Terraform module for a regional deployment","src/acl/pii-sanitizer/ — PII-stripping event filter with allowlist schema validation","src/acl/pii-sanitizer/schemas/global-operational-event.json — strict schema for cross-region events","src/global-dashboard/materializers/ — CQRS read model builders per metric type","src/courier-service/domain/courier-assignment.ts — enforce region invariant in aggregate","src/order-service/domain/order.ts — region binding at order creation","infra/dns/routing-policy.tf — latency-based + geofence DNS routing rules","tests/compliance/pii-leakage.test.ts — automated scan for PII in global event schemas","docs/adr/001-multi-region-sovereignty.md — architecture decision record"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture for regional isolation. CQRS for global read models. Anti-corruption layer for cross-region event sanitization. Cache-aside for resilience. Infrastructure-as-code with parameterized modules. Feature flags per region for gradual rollout. Domain events for intra-region async processing.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not share databases across regions — even read replicas of customer data violate residency requirements","Do not use a global user table with region column — this centralizes PII and creates a single compliance failure point","Do not allow cross-region synchronous calls for any customer-facing operation — latency and coupling","Do not implement global search across customer data — each region's search is self-contained","Do not skip the PII sanitization step for 'internal' dashboards — internal does not mean compliant","Do not use eventual consistency for courier-region assignment validation — this is a hard invariant that must be checked synchronously at assignment time","Do not attempt active-active cross-region replication as a resilience strategy — it fundamentally conflicts with data sovereignty"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":82,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement via regional isolation — this is the standard pattern for GDPR/CCPA multi-region deployments","Cell-based architecture for independent failure domains","PII-stripping ACL at region boundary — well-established compliance pattern","CQRS for global dashboard — textbook use case for read-model projection"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency budget breakdown for < 100ms — depends heavily on specific cloud provider, database choice, and query patterns. Needs benchmarking.","Degraded mode UX specifics — what exactly to show during regional outage requires product input","Global Event Mesh technology choice — Kafka MirrorMaker vs EventBridge vs custom depends on existing infrastructure and team expertise"],"assumptions":["The existing EU deployment is a monolith or modular monolith that can be parameterized for multi-region deployment","Cloud provider supports the three target regions with equivalent service availability","Customer-region binding is determined at registration time and is relatively static","The 100ms latency target is for p95 or p99 within the customer's home region, not cross-region"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using region-sovereign deployments with a thin global coordination layer. Each region (EU, US, APAC) owns its customer and operational data exclusively, enforcing data residency at the infrastructure level. A global mesh synchronizes only non-PII operational metrics and reference data. Region isolation is the primary architectural constraint — every design decision flows from it.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Region Sovereign St
